trip.name: Chill Food and Culture Trip to Hong Kong

days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Exploring Central and Victoria Harbour

timeline.title: Arrive at Hong Kong International Airport
timeline.detail: Touch down at Hong Kong International Airport and make your way to the Express train station to Central.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong International Airport, Chek Lap Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel Icon
timeline.detail: Located in Tsim Sha Tsui, this modern hotel offers stunning harbour views. Unwind and freshen up before exploring.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Icon, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Stroll along Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade
timeline.detail: Walk along the promenade, enjoying the picturesque views of the skyline and Victoria Harbour. Great for photos.
timeline.mapQuery: Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Annie's Kitchen
timeline.detail: Savor authentic Cantonese food including dim sum and roast duck. A popular local spot with a cozy atmosphere.
timeline.mapQuery: Annie's Kitchen, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Symphony of Lights Show
timeline.detail: Watch the famous light and sound show featuring Hong Kong's skyline. Best enjoyed from the harbour side.

timeline.title: Nightcap at Ozone Bar
timeline.detail: Visit the highest bar in the world on the 118th floor of the Ritz-Carlton for amazing city views and a relaxing atmosphere.

localTips: Consider purchasing an Octopus Card for convenient transit throughout Hong Kong.
Dining may be busy after 19:00 — it's best to make reservations where possible.
Respect local customs; say 'thank you' in Cantonese as 'm̀h'gōi' when receiving service.

days.title: Exploring Hong Kong Island
days.subtitle: A day filled with food and history

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australia Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Try the local favorite, Hong Kong-style breakfast with toast, eggs, and milk tea in this bustling café.
timeline.mapQuery: Australia Dairy Company, Jordan, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Victoria Peak
timeline.detail: Take the Peak Tram up to Victoria Peak for breathtaking views of the city. Make sure to arrive early to beat the crowds.

timeline.title: Lunch at Mak's Noodle
timeline.detail: Enjoy a bowl of authentic wonton noodles in this renowned eatery, famous for its delicious flavors and quick service.

timeline.title: Explore Central District
timeline.detail: Wander through the historical and architectural landmarks of Central, including the Man Mo Temple and flagstaff house.

timeline.title: Ferry ride to Tsim Sha Tsui
timeline.detail: Take the famous Star Ferry for a scenic ride across Victoria Harbour, best enjoyed at sunset for the stunning views.

timeline.title: Dinner at Joy Hing Roasted Meat
timeline.detail: Indulge in delicious roasted meats served in a local setting. The char siu is highly recommended by patrons.
timeline.mapQuery: Joy Hing Roasted Meat, Wan Chai, Hong Kong

localTips: Buy online tickets for Peak Tram in advance to avoid long queues.
Try the desserts in local bakeries while exploring the city.
Keep cash handy; some smaller eateries may not accept credit cards.

days.title: Shopping and Cultural Experience
days.subtitle: Unearthing local treasures

timeline.title: Breakfast at a Local Cha Chaan Teng
timeline.detail: Start your day with local breakfast options, including a classic Hong Kong milk tea and pineapple bun.
timeline.mapQuery: Cha Chaan Teng, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Shop at Ladies Market
timeline.detail: Browse through the stalls filled with clothes, accessories, and souvenirs. Practice your bargaining skills while shopping.
timeline.mapQuery: Ladies Market, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Tim Ho Wan
timeline.detail: Savor the world's cheapest Michelin-star dim sum in this beloved local chain. Expect a line but worth the wait!
timeline.mapQuery: Tim Ho Wan, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Wong Tai Sin Temple
timeline.detail: Explore the famous temple known for its fortune telling and beautiful architecture. It's a serene spot in bustling Hong Kong.
timeline.mapQuery: Wong Tai Sin Temple, Wong Tai Sin, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Return to Hotel and Prepare for Departure
timeline.detail: Head back to your hotel, prepare for checkout, and reflect on your amazing trip before heading to the airport.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Icon, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Sister Wah
timeline.detail: Enjoy Sister Wah's famous beef brisket noodles, a must-try before you leave Hong Kong. The flavors are sensational.
timeline.mapQuery: Sister Wah, Causeway Bay, Hong Kong

localTips: Bargaining is expected at street markets; don't hesitate to negotiate.
Use Google Maps for navigating public transport; it's very reliable.
Don’t forget to grab some local snacks for the return trip!

hotels.name: Hotel Icon
hotels.note: Luxury hotel with harbor views, conveniently located in Tsim Sha Tsui.
hotels.address: 17 Science Museum Rd, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

budget.note: Round trip from Penang to Hong Kong
Total estimated for all meals over 3 days
